summary: Second review cycle addressing three WARNING findings from external reviewer berendt. W-001 (doc/code mismatch): docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md now correctly describes the (done bool, err error) signature of finalizeOpenBaoSecrets and documents the stuck-name recording as a side effect on keystone.Status.Conditions via setOpenBaoFinalizerBlockedCondition; the idempotency tuple was corrected from (true, "", nil) to (true, nil). W-002 (RBAC over-privilege): the combined external-secrets.io RBAC rule was split across three surfaces — the kubebuilder markers in keystone_controller.go:115-116, the Helm shared rules partial in _helpers.tpl, and the corresponding Helm tests in clusterrole_test.yaml and role_test.yaml — so externalsecrets receives get/list/watch/create/update/patch while only pushsecrets keeps delete. W-003 (serialized cleanup doubles deletion window): finalizeOpenBaoSecrets was refactored into two sequential passes over openBaoBackupPushSecretNames — pass 1 issues Delete on every PushSecret so ESO cleanup finalizers fire in parallel, pass 2 Gets each and reports blocked on the first still-present. The integration test ordering assertion was relaxed to tolerate both PushSecrets transitioning to Terminating in parallel. All changes are in-scope and improve safety (least privilege), correctness (accurate docs), and wall-clock behaviour (parallel deletion) without introducing new issues. go vet, build, and all controller unit tests pass locally.

tests_checklist.item: Integration test updated to match new parallel-delete semantics
Edge cases preserved: NotFound tolerance on both Delete and Get
Helm test assertions split per-resource (cluster and namespace scope)
Helm unittest not executed in sandbox (helm CLI unavailable) — YAML structure reviewed manually

code_quality_checklist.item: Two-pass loop preserves idempotency and NotFound tolerance contract
Doc comment on finalizeOpenBaoSecrets matches the real two-pass implementation
RBAC rule split carries explanatory comments (CC-0017/CC-0079 rationale) on both sides
Feature ID CC-0079 referenced in all new comments and test assertions
No unrelated refactoring outside the three W-scoped surfaces

security_checklist.item: Least-privilege RBAC: externalsecrets no longer carries delete verb
pushsecrets keeps delete only because finalizeOpenBaoSecrets actively deletes them
No secrets or credentials logged or exposed
No path traversal or injection vectors introduced by the split

architecture_checklist.item: Two-pass structure is the simplest refactor matching the reviewer's suggested fix
Blocked condition still recorded once per pass — no duplication

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated logic across the two passes (each pass has distinct responsibility)
Shared rbacRules Helm partial continues to be the single source for Role + ClusterRole

fail_fast_checklist.item: Non-NotFound errors still propagate from both Delete and Get
Pass 1 aborts on first non-NotFound Delete error (no silent continuation)

defensive_checklist.item: apierrors.IsNotFound tolerance applied uniformly in both passes
setOpenBaoFinalizerBlockedCondition invoked only when a PushSecret is actually still present

suggested_improvements: Optional (out of scope for this cycle, tracked by I-001 in the external review): emit a single Warning event when the finalizer first transitions to the blocked state so alerting tooling that watches Kubernetes events fires on prolonged stalls. The condition + log remain the primary channels.

Optional (out of scope, tracked by I-002): widen hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets to also count a Terminating PushSecret as 'live' when no prior FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets event has been emitted for the current generation, so operator restarts still announce the cleanup once.
next_steps: Re-request external review from berendt once CI green — all three W findings are mechanically addressed and locally verified.
Leave I-001 and I-002 to triage as follow-up tickets; both are observability enhancements and the reviewer explicitly marked them optional.
